source: Condensed from IELTS public Writing Task 2 band descriptors (Task Response, Coherence & Cohesion, Lexical Resource, Grammatical Range & Accuracy).

9.ta: Fully addresses all parts; fully developed position with relevant, extended ideas.
9.cc: Cohesion very rarely attracts attention; skilful paragraphing.
9.lr: Wide, natural, sophisticated control; rare minor slips.
9.gra: Wide range of structures; full flexibility; rare minor errors.

8.ta: Sufficiently addresses all parts; clear well-developed position; ideas relevant and extended.
8.cc: Sequences ideas logically; clear progression; appropriate cohesive devices.
8.lr: Wide resource; skilful use of uncommon items; occasional inaccuracies.
8.gra: Wide range; majority error-free; occasional slips.

7.ta: Addresses all parts; clear position throughout; main ideas extended and supported.
7.cc: Logical organisation; clear progression; range of cohesive devices.
7.lr: Sufficient resource for flexibility and precision; some less common items; few errors in word choice.
7.gra: Variety of complex structures; frequent error-free sentences; good control with occasional errors.

6.ta: Addresses all parts though some more fully than others; relevant position; main ideas relevant but some inadequately developed.
6.cc: Coherent arrangement; overall progression; cohesive devices may be faulty or mechanical.
6.lr: Adequate resource; meaning generally clear; errors do not impede communication.
6.gra: Mix of simple and complex forms; errors occur but rarely impede.

5.ta: Addresses the task only partially; position unclear or repetitive; main ideas limited, not sufficiently developed.
5.cc: Some organisation but lacks overall clarity; inadequate cohesive devices.
5.lr: Limited resource; noticeable repetition; errors may cause difficulty.
5.gra: Limited range; frequent errors; may cause difficulty for reader.

4.ta: Minimal response to task; position unclear; ideas difficult to identify.
4.lr: Basic vocabulary; repetitive; errors may distort meaning.
4.gra: Very limited range; frequent errors; meaning often unclear.
